title: column max length is not stored as specified and accepted at creation time
description:
Tables created with char/varchar/clob/blob/json/url columns with max length of 9999999999 changes the length to 1410065407.
This also happens when creating functions or procedures with char/varchar/clob/blob/json/url arguments.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name CHAR(4000), description VARCHAR(4000), data CLOB, info JSON);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, description, data, info) VALUES (1, 'Test Name', 'This is a test description.', 'Sample CLOB data.', '{"key": "value"}');
SELECT name, description, LENGTH(name) AS name_length, LENGTH(description) AS description_length FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
CREATE FUNCTION test_function(input_name CHAR(4000)) RETURNS VARCHAR(4000) AS 'SELECT CONCAT(input_name, ''_suffix'');
SELECT column_name, data_type, character_maximum_length FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'test_table' AND (column_name = 'name' OR column_name = 'description');
key_bug_features: max length for char/varchar/clob/blob/json/url columns not stored
